---
title: "Final Project"
author: "Saif Elsaady"
date: "2025-02-24"
output: html_document
---

```{r full_analysis, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Read datasets from Downloads directory
bird_surveys <- read_csv("~/Downloads/46_bird_surveys.csv")
bird_observations <- read_csv("~/Downloads/46_bird_observations.csv")
bird_locations <- read_csv("~/Downloads/46_bird_survey_locations.csv")

# Ensure only necessary columns are selected to avoid duplication issues
bird_surveys <- bird_surveys %>%
    select(survey_id, site_code, survey_date, location_type)

bird_locations <- bird_locations %>%
    select(site_code, location_type) # Ensuring location_type remains

# Merge datasets using correct column names
bird_data <- bird_observations %>%
    inner_join(bird_surveys, by = c("survey_id", "site_code", "survey_date")) %>%
    inner_join(bird_locations, by = "site_code", suffix = c("_survey", "_location"))

# Handle potential duplicate location_type column
bird_data <- bird_data %>%
    mutate(location_type = coalesce(location_type_survey, location_type_location)) %>%
    select(-location_type_survey, -location_type_location)

# Convert survey_date to year format for trend analysis
bird_data$year <- as.numeric(format(bird_data$survey_date, "%Y"))


# Figure 1: Bird Species Diversity Over Time

species_diversity <- bird_data %>%
    group_by(year) %>%
    summarise(species_richness = n_distinct(code))  # 'code' represents species identifier

ggplot(species_diversity, aes(x = year, y = species_richness)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue", size = 1) +
    geom_point(color = "red", size = 2) +
    labs(title = "Bird Species Diversity Over Time",
         x = "Year",
         y = "Number of Unique Species") +
    theme_minimal()


# Figure 2: Bird Abundance by Habitat Type
habitat_abundance <- bird_data %>%
    group_by(location_type) %>%
    summarise(total_abundance = sum(bird_count, na.rm = TRUE))

ggplot(habitat_abundance, aes(x = reorder(location_type, -total_abundance), 
                              y = total_abundance, fill = location_type)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
    labs(title = "Bird Abundance by Habitat Type",
         x = "Habitat Type",
         y = "Total Bird Count") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```


```{r statistical_analysis, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(car)  
library(lmtest)  


# Ensure species richness is correctly computed per survey & location
bird_richness <- bird_data %>%
    group_by(survey_id, location_type, year) %>%
    summarise(species_richness = n_distinct(code), .groups = 'drop')

# Ensure location_type is a factor for ANOVA
bird_richness$location_type <- as.factor(bird_richness$location_type)


# Run ANOVA
anova_model <- aov(species_richness ~ location_type, data = bird_richness)
anova_results <- summary(anova_model)
print(anova_results)

# Assumption Check: Homogeneity of Variance (Levene's Test)
levene_test <- leveneTest(species_richness ~ location_type, data = bird_richness)
print(levene_test)

# Post-hoc Analysis: Tukey's HSD Test for pairwise comparisons
tukey_results <- TukeyHSD(anova_model)
print(tukey_results)


# Running the regression model
lm_model <- lm(species_richness ~ year, data = bird_richness)
lm_results <- summary(lm_model)
print(lm_results)

# Assumption Check: Normality of Residuals
residuals_lm <- residuals(lm_model)

# 1. Q-Q Plot for Visual Normality Check
qqnorm(residuals_lm)
qqline(residuals_lm, col = "red")

# 2. Histogram of Residuals
hist(residuals_lm, breaks = 30, main = "Histogram of Residuals", col = "lightblue")

# 3. Kolmogorov-Smirnov Test (For Large Datasets)
ks_test_results <- ks.test(residuals_lm, "pnorm", mean(residuals_lm), sd(residuals_lm))
print(ks_test_results)

# Assumption Check: Homoscedasticity
bptest_results <- bptest(lm_model)  # Breusch-Pagan test
print(bptest_results)

# Residuals vs Fitted Plot
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(lm_model)

# Extracting key results
anova_p_value <- anova_results[[1]]$`Pr(>F)`[1]
lm_p_value <- lm_results$coefficients["year", "Pr(>|t|)"]
lm_r_squared <- lm_results$r.squared

cat("ANOVA Results: p-value =", anova_p_value, "\n")
cat("Linear Regression: R² =", lm_r_squared, ", p-value =", lm_p_value, "\n")

if (anova_p_value < 0.05) {
    cat("Habitat type significantly affects bird species richness.\n")
} else {
    cat("No significant difference in bird species richness across habitats.\n")
}

if (lm_p_value < 0.05) {
    cat("There is a statistically significant trend in bird species diversity over time.\n")
} else {
    cat("No significant trend in bird species diversity over time.\n")
}

```
